channel.title: Guides on The Self-Hosting Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Guides on The Self-Hosting Blog

item.title: Self hosting Stringer, the anti-social RSS reader
item.description: if you're like me, I just want to consume my RSS. I don't want to see any adverts, any fancy recommendations or have a subscription fee. I also want an RSS reader that is pleasant to use. I have dabbl

item.title: Self hosting your own Matrix server on a Raspberry Pi (Updated 2022)
item.description: How to Self Host Matrix, an open source project that publishes the Matrix open standard for secure, decentralised, real-time communication.

item.title: Self-hosting Papercups.io - Open-source live customer chat with Slack integration!
item.description: Papercups is an opensource self-hosted chat window that you can add to your site to interact with your visitors instantly. Papercups gives you the ability to reply directly from Slack or using their b

item.title: Setting up Focalboard - A self-hosted alternative to Trello and Notion
item.description: Focalboard is a self-hosted alternative to tools such as Notion or Trello which you can run on your own instance. Focalboard helps with tracking and organising your own work, as well as across a team.

item.title: Host your static blog for free with the Digital Ocean App Platform
item.description: The Digital Ocean App Platform allows you to host 3 static sites for free. Featuring a Cloudflare CDN, Hugo Static site generator, Github build pipelines, automatic HTTPS, custom domain name and DDoS

item.title: Completely Self-Hosting Plausible.io. A privacy-friendly alternative to Google Analytics
item.description: Plausible is a lightweight, ethical, open-source and privacy-friendly Google Analytics alternative. We're going to show you how to self-host it

item.title: Setting up a Kubernetes cluster (K3s) using Raspberry Pi's and Portainer
item.description: Enter Kubernetes! an open-source platform for container management. I've been experimenting with this and a cluster of Raspberry Pi's for the past week and wanted to share what I've discovered thus fa

item.title: Setting up Portainer. Docker Container management made easy
item.description: Portainer is a lightweight management UI that allows you to easily manage your Docker host or Kubernetes Clusters. Portainer is as simple to deploy as it is to use.

item.title: Setting up a Valheim server using Docker
item.description: I'm going to talk through how to host a Valheim server on a docker image. This will enable you to set up a permanent server for all your friends to enjoy, 24/7.

item.title: How to self-host PiHole, an internet advertising black hole
item.description: This article will cover setting up your own self-hosted PiHole instance. Allowing you to run an internal DNS and block internet advertisements from reaching your machine.

item.title: Archiving ProtonMail Emails on a headless Ubuntu instance
item.description: I wanted to be able to store all of my ProtonMail emails locally for archival purposes, with the intention of running a local content search whenever I needed something retrieving.

item.title: Self-hosting a Wireguard VPN, the easy way
item.description: We're going to cover setting up a Wireguard VPN on your home server or cloud service. For secure remote access to your internal network, or a cheap, secure connection to a cloud service for some incre

item.title: Completely Self-Hosting Bitwarden Password Manager (Updated 2022)
item.description: This article will cover setting up your own self-hosted Bitwarden instance with Docker and configuring ngnix to allow for public exposure for cross device access to your vault.

item.title: How to completely self-host Standard Notes (Updated 2021)
item.description: This is a tutorial on how to self-host Standard Notes, for security, privacy and control of data

item.title: How to Set Up a Headless Syncthing Network
item.description: This is a tutorial on how to setup Syncthing for encrypted P2P file transfers and synchronisation

item.title: How to Route Nginx Through a Child Nginx Configuration - Jumpbox to Web Server Configuration
item.description: Routing nginx through another nginx instance from a central jumpbox server

item.title: Setting Up UFW on Ubuntu Server
item.description: UFW is a program that allows you to internally control ports on your Linux instance. This gives you the ability to forward ports from your machine.
